name: Yeshimbra Asa (Pea Flour Fish)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a dry, heavy pot, stir the onions over low heat until browned.
2. Add 1/3 cup water along with the cayenne, salt, and oil. Simmer for 5 to 10 minutes.
3. Add another 2/3 cup water and the berbere. Bring to a boil, cover, and reduce heat, stirring occasionally.
4. In a separate bowl, mix enough chickpea flour (also called gram or garbanzo flour) with about 1/2 cup water until it forms a thick dough similar to that for rolled cookies. Add extra water a teaspoonful at a time if needed until the mixture adheres to itself and forms a ball.
5. Roll out the dough on a lightly floured surface to a thickness of 1/4 inch and cut into shapes using a cookie cutter (traditional Ethiopian fish shapes work well).
6. Fry the pieces in moderately hot oil in a heavy skillet for 3 to 4 minutes, turning several times until they are crisp and brown.
7. Carefully transfer the fried pieces to the sauce in the pot and simmer gently for 5 or 6 minutes, being careful not to break the shapes.
8. Serve with the sauce.
